content:
The dashboard is now fully responsive. Here is a summary of the changes I made to `client/components/dashboard/dashboard-client.tsx`:

1. **Mobile Sidebar Navigation**: Added a state to toggle a mobile sidebar overlay. The sidebar now stays hidden on small screens (`-translate-x-full`) and slides in smoothly when opened via a new hamburger menu icon added to the header. It acts as a standard sidebar on larger screens (`md:translate-x-0`).
2. **Header Toolbar**: Adjusted padding from `px-8` to `px-4` on mobile devices. Reorganized the layout slightly to accommodate the new mobile menu toggle button while keeping the search bar and quick action button accessible. 
3. **Inspector Drawer**: The slide-out reference details inspector on the right is now responsive. It transitions to `w-full max-w-md` on smaller screens and includes an overlay to prevent background interaction while the drawer is open on mobile.
4. **Bio Link Builder Panel**: Increased the breakpoint threshold for the split view grids from `md` to `lg` (`lg:grid-cols-12`). This ensures the live mobile simulator preview stack nicely underneath the settings form on medium screens, preventing horizontal squishing.
5. **Dynamic Padding**: The main viewport wrapper's padding has been tweaked to use `p-4` on mobile and `md:p-8` on larger screens.

These updates preserve the elegant design on desktops while making the dashboard completely functional and visually pleasing on mobile devices!

content:
I've updated the landing page navbar to be fully responsive!

Here is what changed:
1. I added a **hamburger menu icon** that appears on mobile devices instead of cramming all the buttons into the header.
2. The "EagerMinds" text label next to the logo now gracefully hides on the smallest screens, keeping the header from overflowing.
3. When you tap the hamburger menu, it opens a clean, full-screen **mobile menu overlay**.
4. Inside the mobile menu overlay, you'll find the main navigation links ("Features", "How it works", "Performance") as well as big, thumb-friendly "Sign In" and "Get Started" buttons docked at the bottom.

This creates a seamless mobile experience without breaking the sleek design of the landing page. Check it out and let me know how it looks!

content:
Good catch! I found the issue.

The problem was a slight mismatch in the responsive breakpoints. The "Sign In" and "Get Started" buttons were set to hide on screens smaller than `sm` (640px), but the hamburger menu icon was set to only appear on screens smaller than `md` (768px). This caused an awkward "dead zone" where the layout behaved unpredictably.

I've fixed this by standardizing the breaking points:
- The auth buttons now hide on all screens below `md` width (`hidden md:flex`).
- The hamburger menu icon is now explicitly set to show as a block element on screens below `md` width (`block md:hidden`).

The hamburger menu icon should now be perfectly visible on your mobile screen. Give it a try!

args.title: Layout & Hamburger Fixed

This was causing a horizontal overflow (x-axis scroll). Because the page width was artificially expanded, the header's right edge (and therefore the hamburger icon) was pushed off-screen. content:
I see exactly what happened! The two issues were actually connected.

The absolute-positioned decorative elements (like the background glows and some of the 3D floating canvas items) were bleeding beyond the boundaries of your phone's screen width. This forced the browser to add horizontal scrolling (x-axis overflow).

Because the invisible page width was much wider than your phone screen, the `w-full` header expanded to match that oversized width, which pushed the hamburger menu icon entirely off-screen to the right! 

I've fixed this by adding an `overflow-x-hidden` constraint to the root wrapper of the landing page. This strictly contains all visual elements to your device width. 

As a result:
1. The horizontal scrolling is gone.
2. The hamburger menu icon is now perfectly aligned on the right side of your screen. 

Let me know if it looks perfect on your phone now!

content:
I've updated the back button on both the Sign In and Sign Up pages! 

Previously, it was just a small, subtle circle with an arrow inside. I have transformed it into a clean, modern, pill-shaped button that explicitly says **"Back to Home"** alongside the arrow. It keeps the sleek glass-morphism style (blurred background, subtle borders, and smooth hover effects) but is now much more visible and user-friendly.

The "Verify Email" page already had a prominent "Return to Homepage" button, so that one was left as-is for consistency.

Let me know what you think of the new design!
